221	% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
222	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
223	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
224	% SOURCE QUOTE:
225	% "lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
226	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
227	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
228	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
229	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
230	%     intro Φ
231	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
232	%     infer_instance)"
233	\textit{Source: [Mathlib],
234	\texttt{ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal\_W}, in
235	\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean}, L48--L57.}
236	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
237	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
238	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
239	\(A\).
240	
241	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
242	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
243	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
244	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
245	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
246	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
247	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
248	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
249	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
250	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
251	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
252	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
253	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
254	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
255	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
256	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), a coherent monoidal category, or the stalkwise
257	whiskering apparatus. The route~(e) instance is therefore vestigial and off the
258	critical path.
259	
260	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
261	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
262	is already present in Mathlib: the file
263	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
264	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
265	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
266	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
267	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
268	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
269	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
270	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
271	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
272	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
273	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
274	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. Because the group law
275	is realized through the direct-gluing associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
276	via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and never through
277	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), none of this stalk apparatus is on the critical path;
278	it is retained only to document the superseded route.
279	
280	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
281	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
282	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
283	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
284	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
285	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
286	independently of any upstream timing.
287	
288	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
289	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
290	
291	\begin{definition}
292	
293	\leanok
294	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
295	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
296	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
297	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
298	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
299	  \[
300	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
301	  \]
302	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
303	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
304	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
305	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
306	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
307	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
308	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
309	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
310	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
311	  canonical compatibility
312	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
313	     \;\cong\;
314	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
315	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
316	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
317	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
318	
319	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
320	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
321	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
322	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
323	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
324	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
325	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
326	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
327	\end{definition}
328	
329	\begin{lemma}
330	
331	\leanok
332	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
333	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
335	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
336	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
337	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
338	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
339	  \[
340	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
342	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
343	  \]
344	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
345	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
346	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
347	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
348	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
349	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
350	  \[
351	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
352	    \qquad
353	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
354	  \]
355	  the associator
356	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
357	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
358	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
359	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
360	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
361	\end{lemma}
362	
363	\begin{proof}
364	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
365	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
366	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
367	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
368	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
369	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
370	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
371	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
372	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
373	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
374	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
375	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
376	\end{proof}
377	
378	\begin{remark}
379	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
380	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
381	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
382	        lem:whisker_of_W}
383	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
384	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
385	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
386	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
387	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
388	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
389	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
390	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
391	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
392	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
393	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
394	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
395	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
396	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
397	
398	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
399	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
400	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
401	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
402	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
403	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
404	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
405	  fixed-base presheaves in
406	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
407	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
408	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
409	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
410	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
411	  it.
412	
413	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
414	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
415	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
416	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
417	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
418	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
419	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
420	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
421	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
422	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
423	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
424	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
425	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
426	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
427	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
428	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
429	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
430	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
431	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
432	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
433	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
434	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
435	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
436	\end{remark}
437	
438	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
439	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
440	
441	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
442	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
443	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
444	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
445	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
446	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
447	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
448	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
450	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
451	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
452	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
453	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
454	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
455	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
456	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
457	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
458	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
459	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
460	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
461	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
462	to the off-path route~(e).
463	
464	\begin{lemma}
465	
466	\leanok
467	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
468	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
469	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
470	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
471	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
472	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
473	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
474	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
475	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
476	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
477	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
478	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
479	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
480	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
481	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
482	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
483	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
484	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
485	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
486	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
487	  \[
488	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
489	      \;\;:=\;\;
490	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
491	  \]
492	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
493	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
494	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
495	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
496	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
497	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
498	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
499	\end{lemma}
500	
501	\begin{proof}
502	
503	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
504	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
505	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
506	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
507	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
508	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
509	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
510	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
511	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
512	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
513	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
514	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
515	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
516	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
517	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
518	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
519	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
520	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
521	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
522	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
523	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
524	
525	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
526	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
527	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
528	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
529	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
530	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
531	\end{proof}

533	\begin{lemma}
534	
535	\leanok
536	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
537	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
538	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
539	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
540	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
541	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
542	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
543	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
544	  morphism
545	  \[
546	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
547	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
548	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
549	  \]
550	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
551	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
552	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
553	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
554	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
555	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
556	\end{lemma}
557	
558	\begin{proof}
559	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
560	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
561	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
562	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
563	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
564	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
565	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
566	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
567	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
568	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
569	  isomorphism}
570	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
571	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
572	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
573	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
574	  therefore extension of scalars along
575	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
576	
577	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
578	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
579	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
580	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
581	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
582	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
583	  the abstract pullback functor.
584	
585	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
586	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
587	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
588	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
589	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
590	   \;\cong\;
591	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
592	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
593	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
594	  comparison
595	  \[
596	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
597	      \;\longrightarrow\;
598	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
599	      \;\otimes\;
600	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
601	  \]
602	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
603	
604	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
605	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
606	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
607	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
608	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
609	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
610	  % "\begin{lemma}
611	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
612	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
613	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
614	  % \begin{enumerate}
615	  % \item The functor
616	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
617	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
618	  % \item The functor
619	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
620	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
621	  %  [\dots]
622	  % \end{enumerate}
623	  % \end{lemma}
624	  %  [\dots]
625	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
626	  % of functors, see
627	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
628	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
629	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
630	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
631	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
632	
633	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
634	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
635	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
636	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
637	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
638	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
639	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
640	
641	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
642	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
643	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
644	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
645	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
646	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
647	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
648	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
649	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
650	  \otimes_S N)\).
651	
652	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
653	  \[
654	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
655	  \]
656	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
657	  presheaf-level adjunction
658	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
659	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
660	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
661	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
662	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
663	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
664	  composing the resulting comparison with
665	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
666	
667	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
668	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
669	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
670	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
671	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
672	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
673	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
674	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
675	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
676	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
677	
678	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
679	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
680	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
681	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
682	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
683	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
684	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
685	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
686	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
687	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
688	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
689	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
690	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
691	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
692	\end{proof}

694	\begin{lemma}
695	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
696	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
697	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
698	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
699	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
700	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
701	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
702	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
703	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
704	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
705	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
706	
707	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
708	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
709	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
710	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
711	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
712	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
713	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
714	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
715	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
716	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
717	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
718	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
719	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
720	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
721	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
722	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
723	
724	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
725	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
726	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
727	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
728	  restriction-of-scalars functor
729	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
730	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
731	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
732	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
733	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
734	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
735	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
736	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
737	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
738	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
739	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
740	  morphism but is not syntactically a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).
741	
742	  Together H1 and H2 are the two Mathlib-absent ingredients the linchpin needed; they
743	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
744	\end{lemma}

748	\leanok
749	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
750	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
751	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
752	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
753	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
754	  product: the canonical map
755	  \[
756	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
757	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
758	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
759	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
760	  \]
761	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
762	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
763	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
764	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
765	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
766	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
767	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
768	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
769	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
770	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.

2733	\begin{lemma}
2734	
2735	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with the linear dual (H2$'$)]
2736	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
2737	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}
2738	  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
2739	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M\) be an
2740	  \(S\)-module. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the formation of the
2741	  linear dual: the canonical map
2742	  \[
2743	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \to_S S\bigr)
2744	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
2745	    \bigl(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e M \to_R R\bigr),
2746	    \qquad \varphi \;\longmapsto\; e^{-1} \circ \varphi,
2747	  \]
2748	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence, with inverse \(\psi \mapsto e \circ \psi\). On both
2749	  sides the \(R\)-module structure is the \(S\)-module structure of \(M\) pulled back
2750	  along \(e\), so that \(r \cdot m = e(r) \cdot m\): the left-hand side is the
2751	  \(S\)-linear dual \((M \to_S S)\) regarded as an \(R\)-module by restriction, and the
2752	  right-hand side is the \(R\)-linear dual of the restricted module
2753	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e M\) into the restricted ground ring. This is the dual
2754	  analogue of \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}: it is to the internal hom
2755	  / dual exactly what that lemma is to the tensor product. It is the \emph{H2$'$
2756	  ingredient} of the restriction-compatibility isomorphism of the C-bridge
2757	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} --- the ring-iso/dual commutation that, composed with
2758	  the open-immersion pushforward comparison H1, identifies the dual of a restriction
2759	  with the restriction of the dual.
2760	\end{lemma}
2761	
2762	\begin{proof}
2763	  The two assignments \(\varphi \mapsto e^{-1} \circ \varphi\) and
2764	  \(\psi \mapsto e \circ \psi\) are mutually inverse, since \(e \circ e^{-1} =
2765	  \mathrm{id}_S\) and \(e^{-1} \circ e = \mathrm{id}_R\) as ring homomorphisms, so the
2766	  two composites are the identity on functionals. Each assignment is \(R\)-linear:
2767	  additivity is immediate, and for a scalar \(r \in R\) the defining relation
2768	  \(r \cdot m = e(r) \cdot m\) of the pulled-back module structure makes each linearity
2769	  check a single rewrite --- \(e^{-1}\) (resp.\ \(e\)) intertwines the \(R\)-action on
2770	  the source with the \(R\)-action on the target precisely because \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\)
2771	  are inverse ring homomorphisms identifying \(R\) with \(S\). The forward map is
2772	  therefore an \(R\)-linear bijection, i.e.\ an \(R\)-linear equivalence; packaging the
2773	  two one-sided maps with the inverse identities gives
2774	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}\).
2775	\end{proof}

2777	\begin{lemma}
2778	[The dual of a line bundle is a line bundle]
2779	  \label{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2780	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2781	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2782	        lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
2783	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
2784	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2)
2785	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200--L4213).
2786	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2787	  % "\item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
2788	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
2789	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
2790	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
2791	  % is an isomorphism."
2792	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR,
2793	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible} (item~2).}
2794	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with
2795	  \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\). Then the sheaf-level dual
2796	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} is again locally trivial of
2797	  rank one, i.e.\ \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\).
2798	\end{lemma}
2799	
2800	\begin{proof}
2801	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2802	        lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
2803	  Local triviality of \(L\) furnishes, around each point, an open \(U\) with a
2804	  trivialisation \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The crux is the
2805	  restriction-compatibility isomorphism
2806	  \[
2807	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)\big|_f \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(L|_f\bigr)
2808	  \]
2809	  for an open immersion \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\), built as a \emph{verbatim mirror}
2810	  of the closed linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, with the tensor product
2811	  \(\otimes\) replaced throughout by the internal hom / dual. Three steps are reused
2812	  \emph{unchanged}, because both \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) and the tensor
2813	  \(M \otimes_X N\) are of the form \(\mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{obj}\) of a
2814	  presheaf:
2815	  \begin{enumerate}
2816	    \item reduce restriction to the abstract pullback,
2817	      \((-)|_f \cong (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via
2818	      \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\);
2819	    \item move the pullback inside the sheafification,
2820	      \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \cong
2821	      \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\), via
2822	      \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\);
2823	    \item strip the outer sheafification with \(\mathtt{.mapIso}\), reducing the claim
2824	      to the presheaf-level comparison.
2825	  \end{enumerate}
2826	  The presheaf residual then closes exactly as for the tensor: the open-immersion
2827	  pushforward comparison \emph{H1}, \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
2828	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) (the identification of two left adjoints of the same
2829	  pushforward, \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) together with
2830	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\)), is reused \emph{verbatim}; it is composed
2831	  with the dual analogue \emph{H2$'$} of the tensor's ring-iso commutation, namely the
2832	  ring-iso/dual commutation \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv} along the
2833	  open-immersion structure-sheaf ring isomorphism \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\). Under the
2834	  trivialisation this gives
2835	  \[
2836	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U
2837	      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(L|_U\bigr)
2838	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
2839	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U,
2840	  \]
2841	  the last identification being evaluation at \(1\), the canonical iso of the dual of a
2842	  free rank-one module with itself. Hence \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) is locally trivial of
2843	  rank one.
2844	
2845	  No tensor stalk, and no whiskering \(M \triangleleft \eta\) of the sheafification
2846	  unit, is ever invoked: the comparison reduces to slice / \(\mathtt{Over}\)-reindexing
2847	  under the open immersion (a structural reindexing, not a stalk) together with the
2848	  pure-algebra ring-iso/Hom commutation. Because \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along a
2849	  ring \emph{isomorphism} is an equivalence of module categories, it commutes with
2850	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, -)\) in \emph{both} variances --- so the argument goes through
2851	  identically even though \(\mathtt{dual}\) is contravariant where \(\otimes\) is
2852	  covariant. (Equivalently, this is item~2 of Stacks
2853	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible}, tag 01CR: the dual of an invertible
2854	  module is invertible.)
2855	\end{proof}

2857	\begin{lemma}
2858	
2859	\leanok
2860	[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism]
2861	  \label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}
2862	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}
2863	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in
2864	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\).
2865	  Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of
2866	  \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi\) to \(U_x\) --- the image
2867	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) under the
2868	  restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) ---
2869	  is an isomorphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_x\). Then \(\varphi\) is an
2870	  isomorphism.
2871	\end{lemma}
2872	
2873	\begin{proof}
2874	  Being an isomorphism for a morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules can be
2875	  checked on the underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups, and there it can be
2876	  checked stalkwise. Fix a point \(x \in X\). Choose a preimage \(x' \in U_x\) of \(x\)
2877	  under the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\). By hypothesis the restriction
2878	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) is an
2879	  isomorphism, hence so is its image under any functor --- in particular under the
2880	  stalk-at-\(x'\) functor on the underlying ab-sheaves. The natural isomorphism
2881	  ``restriction commutes with stalks'' (\(\mathtt{restrictStalkNatIso}\)) identifies
2882	  this with the stalk of (the underlying ab-sheaf morphism of) \(\varphi\) at the image
2883	  point \(x\); transporting along it shows that stalk is an isomorphism. As this holds
2884	  at every \(x\), and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups all of whose stalks are
2885	  isomorphisms is itself an isomorphism (stalkwise iso-detection,
2886	  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_stalkFunctor\_map\_iso}\) on \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\)), the
2887	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism of \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism. Finally the forgetful
2888	  functor \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.toPresheaf}\) reflects isomorphisms, so \(\varphi\)
2889	  itself is an isomorphism.

1409	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))

1418	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1419	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1420	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom

1461	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1462	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1463	    (TensorProduct.lift

1470	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1471	        (fun c s φ => by

1543	      congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
1544	        (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq
1545	          (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1546	            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1547	          (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))

1549	    exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key

1562	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1563	
1564	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1565	
1566	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1567	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1568	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1569	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1570	
1571	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1572	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1573	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1574	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1575	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1576	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1577	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1578	
1579	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1580	
1581	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1582	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1583	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1584	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1585	
1586	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1587	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1588	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1589	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1590	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1591	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1592	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1593	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1594	
1595	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1596	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1597	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1598	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1599	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1600	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1601	law is definitional. -/
1602	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1603	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1604	
1605	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1606	
1607	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1608	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
1609	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
1610	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
1611	
1612	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
1613	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
1614	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
1615	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
1616	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
1617	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
1618	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
1619	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
1620	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
1621	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
1622	
1623	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
1624	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
1625	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
1626	
1627	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
1628	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
1629	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
1630	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1631	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1632	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
1633	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)

1635	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1636	
1637	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1638	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1639	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1640	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1641	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1642	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1643	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1644	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1645	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1646	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1647	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1648	
1649	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1650	
1651	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1652	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1653	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1654	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1655	
1656	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1657	
1658	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1659	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1660	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1661	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1662	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1663	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1664	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1665	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1666	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1667	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1668	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1669	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1670	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1671	
1672	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1673	sheaf.**
1674	
1675	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1676	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1677	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1678	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1679	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1680	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1681	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1682	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1683	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1684	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1685	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1686	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

1688	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1689	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1690	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the

1810	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
1811	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
1812	
1813	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
1814	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`

1816	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation

1818	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
1819	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
1820	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
1821	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
1822	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
1823	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
1824	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
1825	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
1826	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
1827	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
1828	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
1829	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
1830	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
1831	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
1832	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
1833	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
1834	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
1835	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
1836	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
1837	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
1838	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
1839	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
1840	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
1841	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

1844	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
1845	
1846	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
1847	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
1848	
1849	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
1850	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
1851	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
1852	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
1853	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
1854	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
1855	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
1856	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
1857	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.

1860	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
1861	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
1862	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
1863	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
1864	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
1865	      definitionally.
1866	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
1867	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`

1869	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite

1872	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)

1877	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_

1893	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
1894	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to

1918	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }

1951	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
1952	
1953	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
1954	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
1955	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
1956	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
1957	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
1958	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
1959	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
1960	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
1961	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
1962	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
1963	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
1964	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
1965	  intro x
1966	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
1967	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
1968	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
1969	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
1970	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
1971	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
1972	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
1973	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
1974	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
1975	    tensorObj_unit_iso

2005	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x

750	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
751	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
752	
753	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
754	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
755	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
756	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
757	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
758	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
759	
760	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
761	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
762	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
763	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
764	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
765	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
766	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
767	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
768	
769	section StalkLinearMap
770	
771	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
772	
773	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
774	
775	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
776	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
777	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
778	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
779	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
780	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
781	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
782	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
783	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
784	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
785	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
786	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
787	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
788	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
789	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
790	  map_smul' r s := by
791	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
792	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
793	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
794	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
795	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
796	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
797	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
798	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
799	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
800	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
801	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
802	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
803	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
804	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
805	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
806	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
807	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
808	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
809	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
810	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
811	      intro z
812	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
813	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
814	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
815	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
816	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
817	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
818	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
819	
820	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
821	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
822	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
823	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
824	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
825	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
826	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
827	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
828	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
829	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
830	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
831	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
832	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
833	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
834	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
835	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
836	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
837	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
838	
839	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
840	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
841	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
842	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
843	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
844	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
845	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
846	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
847	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
848	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
849	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
850	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
851	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
852	
853	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
854	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
855	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
856	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
857	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
858	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
859	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
860	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
861	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
862	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
863	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
864	
865	end StalkLinearMap
866	
867	end PresheafOfModules

869	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
870	
871	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
872	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
873	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
874	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
875	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
876	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
877	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
878	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
879	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
880	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
881	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
882	
883	namespace PresheafOfModules
884	
885	open CategoryTheory Functor
886	
887	section PushforwardNatTrans
888	
889	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
890	
891	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
892	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
893	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
894	
895	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
896	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
897	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
898	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
899	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
900	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
901	  app M :=
902	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
903	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
904	        ext x
905	        dsimp
906	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
907	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
908	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
909	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
910	    ext U x
911	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
912	
913	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
914	    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
915	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
916	
917	end PushforwardNatTrans
918	
919	section PushforwardCongr
920	
921	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
922	
923	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
924	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
925	
926	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
927	rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
928	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
929	presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
930	noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
931	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
932	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
933	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
934	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
935	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
936	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)
937	
938	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
939	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
940	    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
941	
942	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
943	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
944	    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
945	
946	end PushforwardCongr
947	
948	section PushforwardAdj
949	
950	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
951	
952	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
953	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
954	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
955	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
956	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
957	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
958	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
959	
960	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
961	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
962	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
963	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
964	sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
965	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
966	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
967	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
968	  unit :=
969	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
970	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
971	  counit :=
972	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
973	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
974	  left_triangle_components X := by
975	    ext U x
976	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
977	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
978	    dsimp only [id_obj]
979	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
980	    simp
981	  right_triangle_components X := by
982	    ext U x
983	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
984	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
985	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
986	    simp
987	
988	end PushforwardAdj
989	
990	section StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
991	
992	universe v'
993	
994	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C]
995	
996	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
997	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
998	naturality of the given morphism). -/
999	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
1000	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
1001	  haveI := h
1002	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
1003	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
1004	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
1005	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
1006	
1007	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1008	
1009	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
1010	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
1011	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
1012	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
1013	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
1014	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
1015	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
1016	@[implicit_reducible]
1017	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
1018	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1019	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
1020	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
1021	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
1022	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
1023	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
1024	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
1025	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
1026	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
1027	
1028	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
